description_long: This object is a heavily damaged airplane with a fragmented fuselage and broken wings, showing exposed internal structures and scattered debris. The wings are partially intact but display significant tearing and missing sections, with some remnants of red and white paint visible. The fuselage is collapsed and twisted, with the cockpit area barely distinguishable among the wreckage. The tail section is either missing or unrecognizable, and the landing gear appears to be destroyed or absent. Various metallic and composite materials are visible, with some areas showing exposed framework and internal components. The overall appearance suggests a crash site with the airplane in a state of severe disrepair and disintegration.
description: A severely damaged and fragmented airplane with broken wings, exposed internal structures, and scattered debris, resembling a crash site.
description_view_0: This view highlights the collapsed fuselage and fragmented wings, with a focus on the central wreckage and scattered debris.
description_view_1: The left wing is more visible here, showing torn sections and exposed framework, with the main body appearing twisted and broken.
description_view_2: This angle reveals the rear and side of the wreckage, with more visible internal components and a partially collapsed structure.
description_view_3: The right wing and side of the fuselage are prominent, displaying extensive damage and missing sections, with debris spread around.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: low-wing configuration with straight, partially broken wings and no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles
attribute_engine_type: single piston engine in the nose section, partially exposed
attribute_tail_design: tail section is missing or unrecognizable, no visible vertical or horizontal stabilizer
attribute_landing_gear_type: landing gear is destroyed or absent, no visible wheels or struts
attribute_cockpit_placement: forward-mounted cockpit, heavily damaged and partially collapsed, with remnants of an enclosed canopy
